body:
## Problem
MetalForge has 8 top-10 list pages at `/lists/<slug>` with high search intent and rich ranked content:
- `fastest-metal-drummers` — 'who is the fastest metal drummer'
- `death-metal-drummers`
- `most-innovative-drummers`
- `thrash-metal-drummers`
- `drummers-with-budget-friendly-kits` — commercial intent
- `most-expensive-drum-setups` — commercial intent
- `fastest-double-bass-drummers`
- `most-brutal-drum-solos`

`public/llms/lists.md` is a hub summary only. There are **zero per-list LLM markdown files**. Every other major content type now has per-item LLM files (drummers: 61, articles: 62, licks: 55, vs: 24, techniques: 10). Lists are the last content type missing the per-item layer.

AI assistants frequently answer 'who are the fastest metal drummers?' — these per-list files anchor citations to MetalForge.

## Action
1. Create `scripts/generate-llms-lists.cjs` (mirrors `generate-llms-techniques.cjs` pattern)
2. For each entry in `packages/frontend/data/top10Lists.js`, generate `public/llms/lists/<slug>.md`
3. Each file: H1 with ranked keyphrase + intro + ranked entries with rationale + 3 Q&A FAQ + link to live page
4. Add `public/llms/lists/*.md` to `api/sitemap.js` (`...top10Lists.map(l => ({loc: '/llms/lists/'+l.slug+'.md', ...}))`)
5. Update `public/llms/index.md` to reference the `lists/` directory

## Template shape
```markdown
# Top 10 Fastest Metal Drummers — Complete Ranked Guide

The fastest metal drummers in history ranked by technique, BPM records, and legacy.

## Rankings

### 1. George Kollias
[reason from top10Lists.js .entries[0].reason]

### 2. Pete Sandoval
...

## FAQ

**Q: Who is the fastest metal drummer of all time?**
A: George Kollias (Nile) is widely regarded as the fastest...

**Q: What BPM can the fastest metal drummers sustain?**
A: Top-tier blast-beat drummers maintain 250–280 BPM single strokes...

**Q: How do metal drummers build speed without sacrificing power?**
A: Technique-first practice with moeller strokes and rebound control...

[View full ranked list → metalforge.io/lists/fastest-metal-drummers]
```

## Files to touch
- `scripts/generate-llms-lists.cjs` (new)
- `public/llms/lists/*.md` (8 new files)
- `api/sitemap.js` — add lists block (same pattern as techniques/vs blocks)
- `public/llms/index.md` — add `lists/` section

## Expected page count
8 LLM markdown files + 1 hub reference

## Quality gate
- ≥300 words per file; ≥3 FAQ Q&A; internal link to live `/lists/<slug>`; H1 contains core keyphrase (e.g. 'fastest metal drummers')

## Estimated organic uplift
Targets GSC query bucket: 'fastest metal drummers', 'best death metal drummers', 'best thrash metal drummers' — AI citation surface for queries already served by live list pages. Mirrors the proven /llms/technique/ pattern.

## Verify
1. `ls public/llms/lists/` returns 8 .md files
2. `curl https://metalforge.io/llms/lists/fastest-metal-drummers.md` → 200 + markdown
3. Sitemap includes `/llms/lists/fastest-metal-drummers.md`
4. `public/llms/index.md` references `lists/` directory
5. Each file has ≥3 FAQ entries with full Q+A

## Done
8 per-list markdown files in `public/llms/lists/` generated, sitemapped, and referenced in index.md.
title: SEO batch: /llms/lists/<slug>.md per-list LLM markdown (8 files)
